title: Vehicle mitigation system
abstract: A vehicle mitigation system comprises a first portable barrier, a second portable barrier, and a gate assembly comprising a beam. The gate assembly is pivotally attached to the second portable barrier, and the beam is capable of rotating between a closed position and an open position. The beam in the closed position is substantially parallel to a surface on which the first and second portable barriers are positioned. The beam in the open position is no longer parallel to the surface and has been raised vertically away from the surface. The gate assembly has at least one arrestor cable attached between the first portable barrier and the second portable barrier such that the arrestor cable spans a distance between the first and second portable barriers.
claims: 1. A vehicle mitigation system comprising: a first portable barrier; a second portable barrier comprising an arrestor plate extending vertically along a side of the second portable barrier; and a gate assembly comprising a beam, the gate assembly pivotally attached to the second portable barrier, the beam capable of rotating between a closed position and an open position, the beam in the closed position being substantially parallel to a surface on which the first and second portable barriers are positioned, the beam in the open position no longer being parallel to the surface and having been raised vertically away from the surface, the gate assembly having a net formed by a plurality of interconnected arrestor cables attached between the first portable barrier and the second portable barrier such that the net spans a distance between the first and second portable barriers, wherein the plurality of interconnected arrestor cables comprise: a first main arrestor cable extending between the first portable barrier and the second portable barrier and positioned within a passageway of the beam; a second main arrestor cable extending between the first portable barrier and the second portable barrier and disposed below and substantial parallel to the first main arrestor cable; a third main arrestor cable extending between the first portable barrier and the second portable barrier and disposed below and substantial parallel to the second main arrestor cable; a plurality of lateral arrestor cables, each lateral arrestor cable coupled to at least the first main arrestor cable and the second main arrestor cable; the first main arrestor cable is releasably coupled to the first portable barrier via a first hook coupled to an end of the first main arrestor cable; the second main arrestor cable is releasably coupled to the arrestor plate on the second portable barrier via a second hook; and the third main arrestor cable is releasably coupled to the arrestor plate on the second portable barrier via a third hook.
